decisions: Kept all 5 daily logs (04-21 through 04-25): oldest is 04-21 at 5 days old, within 7-day retention window. No deletions warranted.

No content promoted to any tier: reviewed daily/2026-04-25.md contents — only maintenance log entries. Core (MISTAKES.md, PREFERENCES.md, LEARNINGS.md), semantic/, episodic/, and procedural/ all reviewed — no gaps to fill from daily logs.
observations: Brain remains idle since bootstrap on 04-21 — 6 consecutive days of maintenance-only activity with zero user sessions
Daily log directory now contains 5 archived files (04-21 through 04-25) plus TODAY.md, all containing only consolidation notes
First log deletion opportunity will occur on 04-28 when 04-21 ages past 7-day threshold
recommendations: On 04-28, delete daily/2026-04-21.md (will be 7+ days old) — verify its maintenance-only content has no lasting value before deletion
If brain remains idle through 04-30, consider writing a semantic note summarizing the bootstrap-to-dormant pattern for future reference
processImprovements: [self-critique] Six days of identical consolidation cycles with zero user content. The process is mechanically correct but producing no value beyond maintaining the daily log chain. Each report is near-identical to the previous one. The maintenance overhead (reports, commits, guard file updates) is disproportionate to the information being processed.

[proposal] Introduce a 'dormant brain' fast path: if the last 3+ consolidation runs all show zero user sessions and zero promotions, produce a single-line report entry instead of full markdown+JSON reports. Resume full reporting when the first user session occurs. This would reduce report directory bloat on idle brains.

[blocked] Cannot modify maintenance-guard.sh or MAINTENANCE.md — both live in base-brain which is read-only. The activity-aware consolidation threshold proposed in multiple prior reports remains unimplemented.
